events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: PEX crimp ring fails go/no-go gauge. This is a non-compliant installation. The crimp ring is installed but defective/non-compliant. Decision should be fail. Risk is medium/high - a failed crimp can leak. Let me produce the JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 85,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "PEX crimp ring fails go/no-go gauge check, indicating an under- or over-crimped connection that does not meet manufacturer installation requirements.",
    "Defective crimp joint is not leak-tested and cannot be accepted at rough-complete stage."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Cut out and re-terminate the affected PEX connection using a new fitting and crimp ring per manufacturer instructions.",
    "Re-test the replacement joint with the go/no-go gauge before proceeding.",
    "Perform pressure test on the PEX system before close-out."
  ],
  "rationale": "A crimp ring that fails the go/no-go gauge is a non-compliant PEX connection. IPC requires piping installations to comply with manufacturer instructions, and PEX manufacturers mandate that crimp rings pass the go/no-go gauge for a joint to be acceptable. A failed gauge indicates the joint is defective and must be corrected before rough-in is closed out.",
  "references": [
    "IPC - PEX installation requirements and manufacturer compliance",
    "Manufacturer PEX crimp ring installation specifications and go/no-go gauge criteria"
  ]
}
